title: BOM — Product Bill of Materials: Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing (NAICS 333318)

sections.title: Header
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **NAICS Code** | 333318 — Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing |
| **CFJ** | Manufacture and deploy water-using commercial machinery that cleans, treats, purifies, or dispenses aqueous fluids |
| **BOM Scope** | Inline transit-time ultrasonic flow sensor (the Marquardt product) as L4 Fluid Circuit subsystem component — decomposed L4 → L0 |
| **Output Types Served** | All 10 output types in NAICS 333318: commercial washers, tunnel washers, car wash systems, RO/UV water purifiers, water softeners, ice machines, beverage dispensers |
| **Inline Market Size** | ~$273M (2025), ~35% of global ultrasonic flow meter market; CAGR 5.5% [SRC: Market Growth Reports 2025] |

---

sections.title: NAICS Sector Routing
sections.content:
NAICS 333318 is Manufacturing (31–33): **Product BOM applies.** Output products are physical machinery with compositional structure. The Marquardt sensor ends up physically installed **inside** the commercial machinery the customer buys → it belongs in the BOM, not the Process VN.

---

sections.title: BOM Hierarchy
sections.content:
The sensor is L5 (the independently sellable unit). Its four L4 subsystems are decomposed below.

```
L5  Ultrasonic Flow Sensor, inline transit-time, DN20
    |
    +-- L4.1  Acoustic Transducer Subsystem
    |         Two piezoelectric transducer elements (A/B) that emit and receive ultrasonic pulses
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.1.1  Transducer Element Pair
    |   |       L2  PZT Disc + Matching Layer Assembly  (acoustic impedance matching epoxy + filler)
    |   |       L2  Backing Layer Assembly               (loaded epoxy, attenuates rear emissions)
    |   |       L1  Silver electrode contact             (sputtered or screen-printed)
    |   |       L0  Piezoelectric ceramic                ← KEY DESIGN CHOICE (see alternatives)
    |   |       L0  Epoxy resin (matching/backing)
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.1.2  Acoustic Coupling Wedge
    |           L1  Wedge body (defines pulse angle into flow tube)
    |           L0  Engineering polymer (PEEK, Rexolite, or acetal)
    |
    +-- L4.2  Flow Body / Housing Subsystem
    |         The wetted pressure-boundary component that defines DN20 bore and holds transducers
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.2.1  Wetted Flow Tube
    |   |       L2  Machined or injection-moulded tube body with transducer pockets
    |   |       L1  Flow tube body                       ← KEY DESIGN CHOICE (see alternatives)
    |   |       L0  Structural metal or polymer
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.2.2  Process Connection Assembly
    |   |       L1  Threaded end fittings (Rp¾" / G¾" DN20)
    |   |       L1  Sealing faces
    |   |       L0  Same material as flow tube
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.2.3  Outer Enclosure / Head
    |           L1  Electronics housing cover (overmoulded or clipped)
    |           L0  PA66-GF30 or ABS
    |
    +-- L4.3  Electronics & Signal Processing Subsystem
    |         ToF measurement, temperature computation, output signal conditioning
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.3.1  Ultrasonic Measurement PCB
    |   |       L2  Analog Front-End (AFE) Module        ← KEY DESIGN CHOICE (see alternatives)
    |   |       L2  MCU + Communication Module           (pulse output, M-Bus, RS-485, IO-Link)
    |   |       L2  Power Supply Module                  (LDO regulator, EMI filter, TVS)
    |   |       L1  PCB substrate (FR4)
    |   |       L0  FR4 laminate, SAC305 solder, copper foil
    |   |
    |   +-- L3.3.2  Temperature Sensing Module
    |           L1  Dual RTD sensors (PT1000 or PT100)   ← required for heat-meter variant
    |           L1  Signal conditioner
    |           L0  Platinum film/wire, ceramic substrate
    |
    +-- L4.4  Sealing & Retention Subsystem
              Maintains IP65/IP67 integrity and transducer compression at 0–6 bar
        |
        +-- L3.4.1  Transducer Seal Assembly
        |       L1  O-ring seals (transducer pockets)    ← KEY DESIGN CHOICE (see alternatives)
        |       L1  Compression retainer clips or screws
        |       L0  Elastomer
        |
        +-- L3.4.2  Cable / Connector Assembly
                L1  Connector (M12, M8, or pig-tail cable gland)
                L1  Potting/encapsulant
                L0  Polyurethane or epoxy potting compound, tinned copper wire
```

---

sections.title: Alternatives at Key Positions
sections.content:
### L0 — Piezoelectric Ceramic (inside L3.1.1 Transducer Element Pair)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PZT (Lead Zirconate Titanate, hard or soft grades) | 75% | Declining (RoHS pressure) | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2024, Straits Research |
| BNT (Bismuth Sodium Titanate) | 10% | ^ Growing | MEDIUM | BCC Research 2025 |
| KNN (Potassium Sodium Niobate) | 8% | ^ Growing | MEDIUM | BCC Research 2025 |
| Barium Titanate (BT) | 5% | Stable | MEDIUM | analyst estimate |
| AlN / other thin-film | 2% | Emerging | LOW | analyst estimate |

> **Note:** EU RoHS exemption 7(c)-I for PZT in transducers expires under review; 12.3% CAGR for lead-free ceramics (BCC Research). PZT still dominates due to superior d33 coefficient (200–400 pC/N vs 80–150 pC/N for lead-free).

---

### L1 — Flow Tube Body (inside L3.2.1 Wetted Flow Tube)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Stainless steel 316L (machined) | 55% | Stable | MEDIUM | OEM catalog survey (KOBOLD, Endress+Hauser, Burkert) |
| Brass C360 (machined) | 25% | Declining | MEDIUM | OEM catalog survey |
| PVDF (injection-moulded) | 12% | ^ Growing | MEDIUM | analyst estimate |
| Polysulfone / PSU | 5% | Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Bronze / DZR brass | 3% | Declining | LOW | analyst estimate |

> **Marquardt DN20 profile:** Hydronic range (water + max 50% glycol, max 90°C) aligns with SS316L or brass; PVDF excluded by 90°C upper limit.

---

### L2 — Analog Front-End (AFE) Module (inside L3.3.1 Measurement PCB)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| TI MSP430FR604x (integrated USS + MCU) | 35% | ^ Growing | MEDIUM | TI product page; DigiKey article |
| ADI MAX35101/35104 (standalone TDC + AFE) | 20% | Stable | MEDIUM | Analog Devices product page |
| Renesas RL78/I1D (dedicated water meter MCU) | 15% | Stable | MEDIUM | analyst estimate |
| Custom ASIC (large-volume OEM designs) | 20% | Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Other discrete TDC (e.g., ams AS6500) | 10% | Declining | LOW | analyst estimate |

---

### L1 — O-ring Seal Material (inside L3.4.1 Transducer Seal Assembly)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| EPDM (ethylene propylene diene monomer) | 50% | Stable | MEDIUM | standard for water/glycol service |
| FKM / Viton | 35% | ^ Growing | MEDIUM | analyst estimate; preferred at >80°C |
| PTFE encapsulated O-ring | 10% | Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |
| NBR (nitrile) | 5% | Declining | LOW | analyst estimate |

---

### L4 — Acoustic Measurement Architecture (sensor-level design choice, set at L4.1)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Inline dual-path transit-time (A/B, wetted transducers) | 60% | Stable | MEDIUM | Market Growth Reports 2025; product survey |
| Clamp-on transit-time (non-wetted) | 30% | ^ Growing | MEDIUM | Market Growth Reports 2025 |
| Insertion/hot-tap ultrasonic | 5% | Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Multi-path inline (>2 paths, custody transfer) | 5% | Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |

> **Marquardt position:** dual-path inline (wetted). Clamp-on is the structural challenger — no pressure-boundary risk, easy retrofit, but lower accuracy at low flow rates and requires trained installation.

---

sections.title: Component Map — B.4
sections.content:
**Component category: Inline transit-time ultrasonic flow sensor (Marquardt DN20)**

| BOM Position | Output Type | Function at this position | Safety-critical | Key alternatives at position |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| L4.2.x Fluid Circuit | Commercial washer-extractor | Monitors fill volume per wash cycle; enables water consumption reporting | No | Paddle wheel, turbine, Coriolis (cost-up) |
| L4.2.x Fluid Circuit | Tunnel car wash | Monitors water flow in reclaim/recycle loops; triggers replenishment | No | Magnetic flowmeter (conductive water) |
| L4.2.x Fluid Circuit | RO water purification | Measures permeate and reject flow rates; calculates recovery ratio | Yes (membrane protection) | Coriolis (high-accuracy), turbine |
| L4.2.x Fluid Circuit | Commercial ice machine | Monitors inlet water flow rate to control ice slab thickness | No | Float valve (legacy, no measurement) |
| L4.2.x Fluid Circuit | Beverage dispensing | Measures dispensed volume per product for portion control | Yes (regulatory) | Positive-displacement meter, Coriolis |

---

entities.output_type: Ultrasonic Flow Sensor (inline, transit-time, DN20, hydronic)

bom_units.name: Acoustic Transducer Subsystem
bom_units.description: Two piezoelectric transducer elements (A/B configuration) that emit and receive ultrasonic pulses along the flow axis for transit-time differential measurement

children.name: Transducer Element Pair
children.description: Paired PZT or lead-free ceramic discs with matching and backing layers, silver electrodes; one transmit / one receive per measurement cycle

children.name: PZT Disc + Matching Layer Assembly
children.description: Piezoelectric disc bonded to acoustic impedance matching layer (loaded epoxy)

children.name: Backing Layer Assembly
children.description: Loaded epoxy backing attenuates rear acoustic emissions

children.name: Silver Electrode Contact
children.description: Sputtered or screen-printed silver electrode on ceramic face

children.name: Piezoelectric Ceramic
children.description: Ferroelectric ceramic material with high d33 piezoelectric coefficient

children.name: Epoxy Resin (matching/backing)
children.description: Loaded or unloaded thermoset epoxy for matching and backing layers

children.name: Acoustic Coupling Wedge
children.description: Polymer wedge defining ultrasonic pulse angle into the flow bore

bom_units.name: Flow Body / Housing Subsystem
bom_units.description: Wetted pressure-boundary component defining the DN20 flow bore and holding transducers at prescribed angle and spacing

children.name: Wetted Flow Tube
children.description: Machined or moulded tube body with integrated transducer pockets

children.name: Flow Tube Body
children.description: Monolithic or two-piece flow conduit with transducer ports

children.name: Process Connection Assembly
children.description: Threaded end fittings DN20 (Rp¾" / G¾") for inline installation

children.name: Outer Enclosure / Head
children.description: Electronics housing cover, overmoulded or clipped onto flow body

bom_units.name: Electronics & Signal Processing Subsystem
bom_units.description: ToF measurement, temperature computation, and output signal conditioning

children.name: Ultrasonic Measurement PCB
children.description: Analog front-end, MCU, communication, and power supply on single or two-board assembly

children.name: Analog Front-End (AFE) Module
children.description: Time-to-digital converter and analog signal conditioning for ToF measurement

children.name: MCU + Communication Module
children.description: Microcontroller driving output protocol (pulse, M-Bus, RS-485, IO-Link)

children.name: Temperature Sensing Module
children.description: Dual RTD sensors enabling heat-meter calculation (EN 1434)

bom_units.name: Sealing & Retention Subsystem
bom_units.description: IP65/IP67 integrity and transducer compression at 0–6 bar, media compatibility with water and 50% glycol to 90°C

positions.function: Fill volume monitoring per wash cycle
Permeate / reject flow measurement for recovery ratio
